title: agrestis canina
intro: Agrestis canina, commonly known as wild dog rose or dog rose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is known for its health benefits, which include lowering inflammation, reducing stress, and acting as an antioxidant, thereby protecting the body from oxidative damage. The therapeutic actions of Agrestis canina include its use in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues due to its bioactive constituents. The plant contains bioactive constituents including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which contribute to its therapeutic properties.
side_effects: Agrestis canina can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing severe stomach upset, nausea, and vomiting due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Prolonged use of the plant can also lead to kidney damage, diarrhea, and abdominal pain. In severe cases, improper use of agrestis canina can cause respiratory failure, seizures, and even death. It is essential to consult a medical professional before using the plant for any medicinal purpose to avoid these potentially life-threatening side effects.
